subject: Statistics
title: Weather Forecasting: From Art to Science
description: A tiny lesson with a big payoff: Weather Forecasting: From Art to Science.

hook.text: In 1950, the first computer weather forecast took 24 hours to compute a 24-hour prediction. It was barely useful.

buildup.text: Modern weather models divide the atmosphere into a 3D grid and solve physics equations at each point.

discovery.text: Today's 5-day forecast is as accurate as the 1-day forecast was 40 years ago. Steady, measurable progress.

twist.text: Chaos theory limits prediction. Beyond 10 days, small initial errors amplify into total uncertainty.

climax.text: Ensemble forecasting runs many models with slightly different starting conditions to quantify that uncertainty.

punchline.text: Forecasting improved dramatically but chaos caps how far ahead we'll ever see.

quiz.question: Why is weather forecasting fundamentally limited beyond about 10 days?
options: Chaos theory — tiny errors in initial conditions amplify into complete uncertainty
Weather satellites stop working after 10 days
The atmosphere doesn't have patterns beyond 10 days
